methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Terraform Industries is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Casey Handmer that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private-sector peers in Climate & Energy, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, plus one public reference point ( Linanza, ticker LNZA).

A peer-median approach was not executable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the frontier-tech climate sector; no subset of three or more direct peers exists at comparable valuations or development stages. Consequently, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to later-stage advancement. Using the $26M total raised as a valuation proxy, this method implies a valuation of $52M.

The triangulated range is $44M to $60M, with a median of $52M. Key uncertainties center on the absence of narrow peer precedent—the broader Climate & Energy peer set exists but lacks granular valuation homology at this stage and scale. The stage-progression multiple itself (2.0x) reflects general venture patterns but may not account for sector-specific capital intensity, technical risk, or regulatory variables specific to frontier energy infrastructure. Additional clarity on revenue, capital requirements for next-stage scaling, or technical milestones would refine this estimate materially.
